faqs.q: What does interpersonal therapy focus on?
faqs.a: IPT focuses on the link between relationships and mood. It usually centers on one of a few themes such as grief after a loss, conflict in an important relationship, a major role change, or difficulty connecting with others.

faqs.q: How long does interpersonal therapy take?
faqs.a: IPT is time-limited and structured, often running around twelve to sixteen weekly sessions. The time limit and clear focus are part of its design.

faqs.q: Is interpersonal therapy the same as CBT?
faqs.a: No. Both are structured and evidence-based, but CBT centers on thoughts and behaviors while IPT centers on relationships and roles. Some people respond better to one than the other.
